title: Is Shinji sexually attracted to Kaworu split
display_title: Is Shinji sexually attracted to Kaworu split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Oct 24, 2007 10:30 am Alaska Slim wrote: Mr. Hideaki Anno has stated he isn't, and if he isn't, then neither is Shinji. How long must people perverse they're relationship into something it's not, only because in today's society, it is taught love=sex? Why is platonic love such a hard concept?

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Wed Oct 24, 2007 11:04 am I'm also pretty sure that Hideaki Anno was never ordered to pilot a gigantic, bio-mechanical being, that housed the soul of his dead mother, to fight aliens. Does that mean that didn't happen in Evangelion, either?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Oct 24, 2007 11:27 am Szmitten wrote: Alaska is saying that Anno stated that Kaworu isn't gay, not himself.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Oct 24, 2007 11:31 am While I have nothing against the idea of some degree of attraction between Kaworu and Shinji, I frankly don't think they knew each other long enough for that. Also, damn and blast the Newtype Effect and those who keep it alive.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Oct 24, 2007 11:39 am BobBQ wrote: While I have nothing against the idea of some degree of attraction between Kaworu and Shinji, I frankly don't think they knew each other long enough for that.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Oct 24, 2007 11:44 am CitizenGeek wrote: I think he obviously cared for Kaworu an awful lot.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Oct 24, 2007 11:54 am Ah, yes I remember reading that somewhere, actually. Thanks for refreshing my memory! Anyway, I don't think it's as simple as the "Newtype effect" with Shinji and Kaworu. Just go look at episode 24.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Oct 24, 2007 2:37 pm Shinji's relationship with Kaworu, to me, is vague and can be interpreted however the viewer wishes. (I don't have the SAUCE, but I remember reading somewhere that case studies have shown that most heterosexuals have at least a few cases of homosexual attraction during their lives. So it's not outside the realm of possibility.)

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Oct 24, 2007 8:12 pm CitizenGeek wrote: I'm also pretty sure that Hideaki Anno was never ordered to pilot a gigantic, bio-mechanical being, that housed the soul of his dead mother, to fight aliens. Does that mean that didn't happen in Evangelion, either?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Oct 24, 2007 9:42 pm For what it's worth: discussions about Shinji from ANF: http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3829 http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3873

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Oct 25, 2007 12:18 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Here your comparison can apply, as exactly what kind of situation is Shinji in? Due to the weight of what he is doing, the confusion brought on by the truth of Rei's existence, that all his friends are gone, Asuka, someone whom he relied on, is comatose, and not mention Shinji himself is far from "adjusted", don't you think normal social rules have been thrown out the window yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 25, 2007 12:37 pm CitizenGeek wrote: Also, please answer this question: If Kaworu had been Kaworette (a female)

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Thu Oct 25, 2007 12:42 pm CitizenGeek wrote: Did you shower with this friend, did he touch your hand, and after that, did you agree share a bedroom?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 25, 2007 12:49 pm chee wrote: Hikari and Asuka hung out and shared a bedroom, does that make them lesbians?

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Thu Oct 25, 2007 1:09 pm Thankfully, all of that "Hikari & Asuka are Lesbians" nonsense has already been debunked (for the most part anyway). http://evageeks.org/forum/viewtopic.php?t=3137&start=0 <------------

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 25, 2007 1:22 pm They were in a public bath, there is no connotations there. Blushing in anime means what it does IRL, embarrassment. Sometimes the context of the embarrassment is an attraction (clueless guy says something blunt to girl that likes him, girl smiles and blushes while looking away). I've not watch much Yaoi anime, so I don't know the normal conventions of how blushing plays into those relationships.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Thu Oct 25, 2007 1:49 pm Mr. Tines wrote: a) Kaworu is a unisex name

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Oct 25, 2007 2:37 pm CitizenGeek wrote: Given that sexuality plays a large part in Evangelion, you still think Shinji in a public bath with his friend, while both are naked, means nothing?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 25, 2007 2:50 pm CitizenGeek wrote: Mr. Tines wrote: a) Kaworu is a unisex name

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Oct 25, 2007 6:26 pm Mr. Tines wrote: "Spelt" is not really the right word to use in this context. Anno, for reasons known only to himself, uses phonetic spelling in katakana for all given names in NGE; so in that contaxt it would be カヲル whatever.
summary:
[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Oct 24, 2007 10:30 am Alaska Slim wrote: Mr. Hideaki Anno has stated he isn't, and if he isn't, then neither is Shinji. How long must people perverse they're relationship into something it's not, only because in today's society, it is taught love=sex? Why is platonic love such a hard concept?

[]:             Quote Post by S
